Remove JS text truncation in table formatters, let CSS line-clamp handle it

Removed the 60-char details truncation and 24-char secret truncation from
JS formatters — CSS 3-line clamp handles visual truncation, and the removed
JS truncation was preventing tooltip overflow detection from triggering.
Also improved tooltip handler with dual detection: JS truncation check
(tip length vs visible text) and CSS clamp check (unset clamp to measure
true scrollHeight).

Fix tooltip overflow detection for line-clamped cells

Now temporarily unsets the clamp to measure true content height,
then compares against clamped height to decide if tooltip is needed.

commit.message:
Fix graph ordering: WEB01 after DC01, access→host edges

Pivot edges now prefer host→host (not access→host) so BFS layer
assignment places pivot destinations after the source host. Added
unconditional access→host edges so the chain flows correctly:
cred → access → host → [pivot] → next host.

Fix graph: WEB01 as pivot dest, table cell wrapping + tooltips

- pivotDestHosts built from actual edge rendering (replaces broken
  pivotDests built from raw destination text that never matched hosts)
- Fuzzy pivot source/destination matching: descriptive text like
  "DC01.pirate.htb (10.129.244.95) - gMSA..." now matches host IPs
  and subnet prefixes (192.168.100.0/24 matches host 192.168.100.2)

commit.message:
Add via_access_id provenance tracking to kill-chain graph

Schema v3→v4 migration adds via_access_id FK on credentials and vulns
tables, linking discoveries to the access session that found them.
State-server add_credential/add_vuln accept the new parameter.
State-viewer graph uses via_access_id for explicit edge inference
(access→vuln, access→cred) with heuristic fallback for NULL values.
Access nodes are pre-created before vuln/cred processing so references
resolve correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: red-run
repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Fix provided-cred chain ordering and reduce edge crossings

Provided-cred chain detection was fragile — parsed node labels to
find hosts. Now tracks hostsViaProvidedCred directly during access
node creation when matching against a provided credential. This
ensures attacker->cred->access->host->downstream ordering instead
of cred appearing as a sibling of the host.

Also broadened cred->access matching to check access.method for
the credential username (catches cases where username formats
differ between cred and access records).

Added barycenter heuristic for node ordering within layers (3
passes). Nodes in each layer are sorted by the average Y position
of their parent nodes, which reduces edge crossings — e.g., the
DC->gMSA_ADCS edge no longer has to route through credential boxes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

Wider graph nodes, drop severity subtitle from vulns

truncation raised to 26 chars. Vuln nodes no longer show severity

cleaner with more room for vuln titles.

Remove blocked items from kill-chain graph

Keep them in the Blocked table only. Removes blocked node creation,

repository.name: red-run
repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Fix kill-chain ordering: provided creds flow attacker->cred->access->host

When a credential is provided (source matches "pentest", "provided",
etc.), the chain now flows: Attacker -> cred -> access -> host ->
downstream findings. Previously the host got a direct edge from
Attacker, making the cred appear as a sibling instead of an ancestor.

Changes:
- Skip attacker->host edge for hosts reachable via provided-cred chain
- Add access->host bridge edges so downstream nodes (vulns, pivots,
  blocked) that connect from the host node stay in the chain

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: red-run
repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Prune dead-end vulns from graph, link provided creds from Attacker

Graph now only shows vulns that chain into something downstream
(credential, access). Dead-end findings that didn't lead anywhere
are pruned from the graph but remain in the Vulns table.

Credentials whose source matches "provided", "scope", "pentest",
"given", "initial", or "pre-engagement" now link from the Attacker
node instead of from a host — reflecting that they were supplied
as part of scope, not discovered through exploitation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: red-run
repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Render pivots as labeled edges instead of nodes

Pivots are relationships, not entities — they now render as styled
edges between source access/host and destination host. Edge label
shows method + status (e.g. "sshuttle", "chisel (identified)").
Exploited pivots are solid green, identified are dashed yellow,
blocked are dotted red. Removes the uninformative column of
identical "pivot" boxes.

Edge legend labels updated: Exploited/Identified/Blocked.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: red-run
repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Improve kill-chain graph: subtitles, legend, manual refresh

Graph nodes now show two lines — label + contextual subtitle:
- Hosts: IP + role/OS
- Vulns: title + severity (color-coded by severity level)
- Credentials: user + secret_type (cracked)
- Access: user@host + type|privilege
- Pivots: method + source->destination (instead of generic "pivot")
- Blocked: technique + truncated reason

Adds a legend bar below the graph showing node shapes/colors and
edge styles (confirmed/pending/blocked).

SSE auto-refresh now only updates cards + tables. Graph only redraws
on explicit Refresh button click to prevent disruptive reloads.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add state-viewer web dashboard with kill-chain graph

Read-only browser dashboard for engagement/state.db served at localhost:8099.
Stdlib-only Python HTTP server with inline HTML/CSS/JS — no dependencies.

Features:
- Kill-chain SVG attack graph (hosts, vulns, creds, access, pivots, blocked)
- State tables with sorting, filtering, collapsible sections
- SSE live updates (events every 2s, full refresh every 10s)
- Token-based auth for remote access (generate-token.sh binds 0.0.0.0)
- HMAC-signed session cookies, Bearer header support for API clients
- Graceful degradation when DB doesn't exist yet

Closes #64

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Dashboard reliability fixes, JSONL agent discovery, event watcher timeout

Dashboard:
- Fix crash on embedded null characters in agent output
- Fix stale agents reappearing on dashboard restart (5-min mtime filter)
- Fix agent browser discovery when task symlinks are not created
- Discover agents from ~/.claude/projects/*/subagents/ JSONL dirs (reliable
  source, always created by Claude Code) in addition to task symlinks
- Limit agent browser to last 24 hours
- Fix duplicate panes when .dashboard path and auto-discovery resolve to
  same JSONL file (resolve symlinks for dedup)
- Filter non-symlinks from tasks dir to exclude event watcher output

Hooks:
- Increase event watcher timeout from 10 to 30 minutes (long technique
  chains can take 15-25 min)
- Add credential-cracking-agent and pivoting-agent to SubagentStop hook

Orchestrator:
- Update dashboard path documentation to prefer JSONL paths from agentId

Tests:
- Add SubagentStop hook coverage test for all 11 agent types

commit.message:
perf: eliminar N+1 en ventas, output cache en catálogos, code splitting frontend

Backend:
- VentaService: pre-carga productos y stocks en lote con ToDictionaryAsync antes del
  foreach de líneas (de 4N queries a 2 fijas para N items)
- OutputCache 5min en GET categorías, impuestos, sucursales (varía por query params)
- OutputCache 1h en GET países, ciudades, tipos de impuesto
- UseOutputCache() en pipeline de Program.cs
- Eliminados logs de debug de SucursalesController
- Migración AgregarIndicesRendimiento2: ix_documentos_electronicos_sucursal_estado
  e ix_documentos_electronicos_venta_tipo para queries de facturación

Frontend:
- Code splitting con React.lazy en todas las rutas secundarias (reportes, productos,
  compras, traslados, configuración, facturación, etc.)
- Rutas críticas (Dashboard, POS, Ventas, Cajas, Inventario) mantienen carga inmediata
- Suspense global con LinearProgress como fallback

commit.message:
feat: redesign sign-in — 3-step flow (role → method → email), auto-detect portal

Replaces confusing 5-icon portal selector with Compass-style stepped flow:
1. Choose role: Agent or Client (Buyer/Renter/Seller/Landlord)
2. Choose method: Google, Facebook, Apple (coming soon), or Email
3. Email + password form with forgot password

Login API now supports portalType "auto" — tries Agent table first, then
Lead table, and reads portal_role from DB. No need for users to specify
their exact role at login time.

"Open to senior AI roles" text that was displayed with a map-marker
icon in the sidebar.

Closes #24

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: cron mode with unified daemon and per-job policy (#63)

* docs: add cron mode design doc

Design for scheduled agent tasks with per-job policy compilation,
persistent workspaces, unified daemon, and Signal escalation integration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: implement cron mode with unified daemon and per-job policy

- SessionManager: extracted from SignalBotDaemon, owns session map and
  escalation state; cron sessions don't steal Signal focus
- CronScheduler: built-in cron expression parser, skip-on-overlap
- HeadlessTransport: autonomous single-task session transport
- JobStore: CRUD for job definitions and run records under ~/.ironcurtain/jobs/
- PipelineRunner: reusable pipeline abstraction with constitutionKind
  discriminant; task-policy mode generates a tool whitelist from English
- compileTaskPolicy: per-job policy compilation with mcpServers from config
- IronCurtainDaemon: unified daemon composing Signal + scheduler + SessionManager
- loadGeneratedPolicy: split into policyDir + toolAnnotationsDir
- CLI: ironcurtain daemon [add-job|list-jobs|run-job|remove-job|
  disable-job|enable-job|recompile-job|logs]
- ironcurtain bot remains as alias for ironcurtain daemon

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: simplify cron/daemon code after review

- Use instanceof BudgetExhaustedError instead of string matching
- Replace resolve(getJobWorkspaceDir(), '..') hacks with getJobDir()
- Remove existsSync TOCTOU anti-pattern in deleteJob (rmSync force:true handles missing dirs)
- Remove DEFAULT_CRON_BUDGET alias, use CRON_BUDGET_DEFAULTS directly
- Normalize Sunday (7→0) in parseCronExpression instead of per-tick Set allocation
- Use parseCronExpression directly in cron validate callback (no temp scheduler needed)
- Extract formatRunOutcome helper to eliminate 3 repeated ternary chains
- Remove unused onEscalation/onEscalationExpired/onDiagnostic from HeadlessTransportOptions
- Run session shutdown concurrently with Promise.allSettled
- Remove redundant per-run mkdirSync (workspace created at job-add time)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add git repo pre-cloning for cron jobs

Clone the repo at add-job time so policy compilation sees the real
codebase; refresh with fetch + reset --hard before each run so
tracked files stay at remote HEAD while untracked agent artifacts
are preserved.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: open $EDITOR for multi-line task description in add-job wizard

Replace the single-line clack text prompt with a $VISUAL/$EDITOR
invocation (falling back to nano). The temp file is pre-populated
with instructional comment lines; anything starting with # is stripped
on save. Empty saves re-open the editor until a non-empty description
is provided.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: retry git clone on failure instead of aborting wizard

When the clone fails, show the error and prompt for a corrected URI
rather than calling process.exit(1). Leaving the corrected URI empty
skips cloning and continues with job creation. This preserves the
task description (and all other wizard state) across a bad URI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add review-and-edit loop to add-job wizard

After collecting all inputs, show a summary and let the user select
any field to re-enter before confirming. Handles re-editing the ID
(with existence re-check), schedule (with validation), task (reopens
editor preserving existing text on empty save), git repo, and
notifications. Workspace creation and cloning happen only after
confirmation, so no work is lost if a field needs fixing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: replace sequential wizard with review-loop-first UX; add edit-job

add-job now jumps straight into a review/edit screen pre-filled with
auto-generated defaults (random ID, daily 9am schedule, empty task).
The user selects any field to edit rather than stepping through every
prompt in order. Confirm is blocked until the task description is set.

edit-job reuses the same review loop, seeded with the existing job's
values, with the ID field hidden (can't rename an existing job).
If the task changes, policy is recompiled automatically.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: normalize annotation args to arrays; save job before policy compile

Annotations generated by older pipeline versions stored single roles as
a plain string rather than a one-element array, causing a
"roles.join is not a function" crash in buildTaskCompilerSystemPrompt.
Normalize args values to arrays when loading annotations so both old
and new formats are handled transparently.

Also reorder add-job and edit-job to saveJob() before compileTaskPolicy()
so the job definition is never lost on compilation failure. On failure,
print the job ID and the recompile-job command to recover.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve StoredToolAnnotationsFile at load boundary for pipeline

Add resolveStoredAnnotationsFile() to argument-roles.ts and a
loadToolAnnotationsFile() loader to pipeline-shared.ts that resolves
conditional role specs to their default roles before handing data to
the compilation pipeline.

pipeline-runner.ts now calls loadToolAnnotationsFile() instead of
loadExistingArtifact<ToolAnnotationsFile>() directly, so it only ever
sees the flat ToolAnnotation shape. StoredToolAnnotationsFile remains
an implementation detail of the annotator and policy engine.

This fixes the "roles.join is not a function" crash caused by
ConditionalRoles objects in tool-annotations.json reaching code that
expected plain ArgumentRole[] arrays.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: apply loadToolAnnotationsFile to compile.ts

compile.ts had the same unsafe loadExistingArtifact<ToolAnnotationsFile>
cast as pipeline-runner.ts -- both would crash on ConditionalRoles objects
after the conditional argument roles merge. This was an oversight in the
master commit that changed the on-disk format without updating either
pipeline loading site.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: separate taskDescription from taskConstitution in cron jobs

Split the single `task` field into two required fields:
- taskDescription: sent to the agent as its work prompt
- taskConstitution: used as the policy compilation input

The review loop shows both fields separately, blocks confirm until
both are set, and pre-fills the constitution editor with
"The agent is allowed " as a starting point. Policy recompilation
in edit-job now triggers on constitution changes only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add github-repo role and fix lists extraction in policy engine

Add `github-repo` as an identifier-category resource role for GitHub
repository name arguments. This allows policy rules to independently
constrain both owner and repo (e.g. allow only provos/ironcurtain)
instead of relying on the owner constraint alone.

Fix a bug in PolicyEngine.ruleMatches() where list conditions targeting
a role other than the current evaluatingRole would incorrectly use
[evaluatingRole] for argument extraction. The correct behavior is to
scope extraction to evaluatingRole only when the list condition's own
roles include it; otherwise use the condition's declared roles. This
fix makes multi-identifier rules (github-owner + github-repo) work
correctly.

`annotate-tools` must be re-run to reclassify `repo` args from `none`
to `github-repo` in the generated tool-annotations.json.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: document known gap in URL role enforcement (Issue 2)

The structural domain gate is the intended primary defense against
compiled allow-rules that lack URL constraints (e.g.
allow-git-push-default-remote). These rules fire as no-remote fallbacks
but cannot distinguish an explicit unauthorized URL from an absent one.

The git server's allowedDomains is currently disabled due to a Linux
SSH/sandbox-runtime limitation (see mcp-servers.json _comment). Until
that's resolved, a gap exists where explicit unauthorized remotes could
match broad allow rules. Option A (skip no-constraint allow rules when
concrete URL values are present) is the correct long-term fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(policy-engine): sandbox-resolve git_clone write-path when URL roles present

Previously, partial sandbox resolution for path roles was filesystem-only,
causing git_clone to deny when the destination path was inside the sandbox.

The fix extends sandbox resolution to git tools only when URL roles are also
present (urlArgs.length > 0). This targets git_clone (write-path +
git-remote-url) without affecting repo-locator-only tools like git_status,
git_branch, and git_add which must fall through to compiled rule evaluation
so that tool-specific escalation rules can fire.

- git_clone path in sandbox → write-path sandbox-resolved; git-remote-url
  goes to compiled rules (URL-allow rule can permit the clone)
- git_status/git_branch/git_add/git_commit → no URL roles → no sandbox
  extension → fall through to compiled rules as before

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(policy-engine): enrich git requests by resolving default remote URL

When git_push/pull/fetch is called without an explicit remote arg, the
policy engine previously saw no URL and couldn't apply domain checks or
compiled domains conditions. This closes Issue 2.

resolveDefaultGitRemote() in domain-utils.ts resolves the repo's default
remote via git config (branch.<name>.remote → git remote get-url), falling
back to 'origin'. Uses git config directly so it works even when the remote
has never been fetched (no remote-tracking refs required).

PolicyEngine.enrichGitRequest() injects the resolved URL for any absent
git-remote-url arg before structural and compiled rule evaluation, so:
- The structural domain gate fires for implicit pushes to untrusted remotes
- Compiled rules with domains conditions match correctly
- The compiler can write domain-constrained allow rules instead of
  unconstrained fallback rules (e.g. allow-git-push-github with
  domains: ['github.com'] instead of allow-git-push-default-remote)

Tests: new test/domain-utils.test.ts covers resolveDefaultGitRemote with
real git repos (tracking priority, origin fallback, SSH URLs, safe-fail).
New describe block in policy-engine.test.ts covers enrichment end-to-end
including structural domain gate and compiled domains condition paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(scenario-generator): always require explicit git-remote-url in scenarios

The policy engine resolves the default git remote from the filesystem at
runtime (enrichGitRequest), but that resolution does not run during policy
verification. A scenario with no remote arg produces zero URL args, so
domain-constrained compiled rules (domains condition) cannot match and
appear to fail incorrectly, triggering unnecessary repair loops.

Add an explicit instruction to buildGeneratorSystemPrompt: for git-remote-url
annotated arguments, always supply a concrete URL value in scenarios. Applies
to both the compile.ts and pipeline-runner.ts paths (both call the same
buildGeneratorSystemPrompt function).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: updated tool annotations

* feat(policy-engine): hierarchical domain matching and git sandbox resolution

Extend extractGitDomain() to return hostname/owner/repo (e.g.,
github.com/provos/ironcurtain) instead of just hostname, enabling
repo-level domain rules in compiled policies. Update
domainMatchesAllowlist() for hierarchical matching: hostname-only
patterns match any repo on that host, patterns with / require exact
match.

Add write-history and delete-history to SANDBOX_SAFE_PATH_ROLES so
git tools like git_fetch/git_pull are correctly sandbox-resolved.

Extend structural sandbox auto-allow from filesystem-only to all git
tools, removing the requirement for URL roles to be present. This
ensures git_commit, git_branch, git_stash, etc. are auto-allowed when
all paths are within the sandbox.

* feat(daemon): integrate session mode resolution into job and daemon commands

Updated the `runJobCommand` and `runDaemonCommand` functions to resolve session modes dynamically using the new `resolveSessionMode` function. This change allows for more flexible session management by loading configuration settings and adapting the daemon's operational mode accordingly. The `IronCurtainDaemon` now utilizes the resolved session mode instead of a hardcoded 'builtin' mode, enhancing the overall functionality and adaptability of the daemon.

* fix(session): forward policyDir in Docker mode and disable git sandbox

Docker sessions dropped the per-job policyDir when calling
buildSessionConfig, causing cron jobs to load the global compiled
policy instead of the job-specific one. All write operations were
escalated and denied since no human is present to approve them.

Also disable OS-level sandboxing for the git MCP server because
sandbox-runtime always uses --unshare-net on Linux with no way to
selectively allow network access, breaking git push/fetch/clone.

* feat(git): enrich git tool arguments with working directory path

Enhance the `handleCallTool` function to automatically inject the working directory path for git tools when the `path` argument is omitted. This change ensures that the policy engine can correctly resolve sandbox containment and default remote URLs.

* feat(sandbox): tool description hints and __getToolInterface auto-correction

Add data-driven tool description hints (JSON config) that append usage
guidance to MCP tool descriptions before exposing them to agents. This
helps agents use structured parameters correctly (e.g. createBranch: true
instead of -b flag for git_checkout).

Patch __getToolInterface in the V8 sandbox to auto-correct common naming
mistakes (missing "tools." prefix) and return helpful error messages with
suggestions instead of null for unknown tool names.

Update the system prompt to use explicit callable name format with
concrete examples for __getToolInterface.

* fix(security): validate workspace paths, wire cron escalations, reject dangerous git URIs

- Validate job workspace against protected paths in both addJob() and
  executeJob(), preventing sandbox containment bypass via hand-edited
  job.json
- Wire handleCronEscalation as onEscalation callback in cron sessions
  and track escalation counts in run records
- Add git URI allowlist rejecting ext:: protocol and other dangerous
  schemes, with GIT_PROTOCOL_WHITELIST defense-in-depth

* fix(security): add job store file locking and policyDir containment check

- Advisory file lock (mkdir-based, POSIX-atomic) for saveJob() and
  saveRunRecord() to prevent concurrent write corruption across processes
- Validate policyDir resolves under IronCurtain home directory before
  loading policy artifacts, preventing arbitrary policy injection

* refactor: deduplicate isPidAlive, simplify file-lock, remove unnecessary locking

- Import shared isPidAlive from listener-lock.ts instead of duplicating
  in file-lock.ts and session-registry.ts
- Replace fragile unlinkSync+rmdirSync with rmSync({recursive}) in
  forceRelease
- Remove unnecessary file lock from saveRunRecord (unique filenames
  cannot collide), revert to synchronous
- Remove unsafe type cast on GIT_SAFE_ENV
- Reuse isEqualOrInside in validatePolicyDir instead of inline check

* fix(git-sync): log tracked-file changes before hard reset

The hard reset discards any modifications the agent made to tracked
files between runs. Log a git diff --stat before resetting so there
is an audit trail of what was discarded.

* feat(cron): record discarded git changes in run record

syncGitRepo now returns the diff --stat of tracked-file changes
discarded by the hard reset. The daemon captures this and stores it
in the RunRecord as `discardedChanges`, providing an audit trail of
agent work lost during pre-run sync.

* refactor: deduplicate and simplify code across feature branch

- Deduplicate createPipelineModels → delegates to createPipelineLlm
- Extract shared JOB_ID_PATTERN constant (was duplicated regex)
- Extract loadJobOrExit helper in job-commands.ts (7 identical blocks)
- Extract requireJobIdArg helper in daemon-command.ts (7 identical blocks)
- Use parseCronExpression + getNextFireTime directly instead of
  instantiating throwaway CronScheduler with timer side effects
- Fix escalationsApproved: const → let (was always 0, never mutable)
- Move GIT_SAFE_ENV inside syncGitRepo to avoid stale env snapshot
- Fix TOCTOU in file-lock: mkdirSync first, check staleness on EEXIST
- Reuse resolved paths in policy engine to avoid double resolveRealPath
- Merge runEnableJob/runDisableJob into shared runSetJobEnabled

* fix(security): address review feedback on policy engine and git safety

- Move enrichGitRequest after structural invariants so protected-path
  checks run before any filesystem access (git config reads)
- Skip git remote enrichment when path arg is absent to avoid resolving
  from the trusted process working directory
- Restrict structural sandbox auto-allow to filesystem server only;
  git operations have non-filesystem side effects (network, credentials)
  that path containment does not mitigate
- Preserve configured toolAnnotationsDir when policyDir is set
- Add -- separator before URI in git clone for argument injection defense
- Validate cron step >= 1 to prevent infinite loop on */0
- Fix headless transport comment about escalation handling
- Use mkdirSync instead of execFileSync('mkdir') in test

* feat(session): disable auto-approver for headless cron sessions

Cron sessions have no interactive user context, so intent-matching
against a user message is meaningless. Add disableAutoApprove option
to SessionOptions and wire it through buildSessionConfig to override
userConfig.autoApprove.enabled=false for daemon-executed jobs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(signal): unify session management via shared SessionManager

SignalBotDaemon now accepts a SessionManager via constructor injection
instead of maintaining its own internal session map and label counter.
Both Signal and cron sessions share the same SessionManager, enabling
unified escalation routing: `approve #3` from Signal correctly resolves
escalations from either session type.

Key changes:
- Remove internal sessions map, nextLabel, autoSwitchCurrent from SignalBotDaemon
- Delegate all session state to injected SessionManager
- Add byKind() to SessionManager for filtering by source type
- Guard message routing and /switch to Signal sessions only
- Late-bind session label in transport callbacks (assigned after register)
- Shutdown Signal daemon before remaining sessions in IronCurtainDaemon

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(session): simplify session configuration and improve signal session retrieval

- Consolidate parameters in buildSessionConfig to accept a single options object, streamlining session configuration.
- Optimize findMostRecentSignalLabel method in SessionManager to use array mapping for improved readability and performance.
- Update SignalBotDaemon to use a private sessionManager, enhancing encapsulation.

These changes enhance code clarity and maintainability while ensuring consistent session management across the application.

* refactor(pipeline): delegate compile.ts to PipelineRunner

compile.ts main() is now a thin 85-line CLI wrapper that loads config,
prints the banner, and delegates the compile-verify-repair loop to
PipelineRunner. Extracted MCP connection helpers into mcp-connections.ts
and moved resolveRulePaths/mergeReplacements to pipeline-shared.ts.
Eliminates the duplicate pipeline implementation (was 1037 lines).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(pipeline): clean up dead exports, fix TOCTOU, parallelize MCP connections

- Remove unused re-exports from compile.ts and pipeline-shared.ts
- Remove dead constitutionHash field from PipelineConfig
- Remove unused protectedPaths param from compilePolicyRules
- Fix TOCTOU in loadExistingArtifact (drop existsSync, use try/catch)
- Parallelize MCP server connections via Promise.all
- Pass preloaded tool annotations to PipelineRunner to avoid duplicate read
- Fix lint: const escalationsApproved, remove unused Session import

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(daemon): add Unix domain socket for live CLI-to-daemon communication

CLI subcommands (remove-job, enable-job, disable-job, recompile-job,
run-job, list-jobs) now detect a running daemon via ~/.ironcurtain/daemon.sock
and forward commands to it. Falls back to direct filesystem operations
when no daemon is running.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(daemon): simplify control socket and deduplicate job display

- Remove double socket round-trip: tryForwardToDaemon calls
  sendControlRequest directly instead of pinging first
- Remove dead probe connection and redundant existsSync in socket cleanup
- Fix TOCTOU: sendControlRequest tries connecting without pre-checking
- Fix leaked timer: clear connect timeout on successful resolution
- Add multi-request guard in onConnection to ignore duplicate data
- Extract shared printJobEntry from runListJobs/formatDaemonJobList
- Add typed DaemonJobListEntry interface replacing Record<string, unknown>
- Fix lint: non-null assertion, floating promise, unused import,
  unnecessary conditional

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(cron): add functional unit tests for cron-mode components

Add 102 tests across 5 files covering job store, cron scheduler,
session manager, headless transport, and cron prompt augmentation.
Tests use real filesystem operations with minimal mocking — no
Docker or LLM calls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style: fix prettier formatting across 12 files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(security): address 5 review findings from Copilot

1. resolveStoredAnnotationsFile: use extractDefaultRoles() instead of
   evaluateConditionalRoles({}) to avoid 'is:absent' clauses matching
   empty args and producing non-default role sets.

2. enrichGitRequest: gate git config reads on sandbox containment so
   attacker-controlled paths outside allowedDirectory cannot trigger
   filesystem reads from the trusted process.

3. validatePolicyDir: use realpathSync to canonicalize paths before
   containment check, preventing symlink escapes (e.g.,
   ~/.ironcurtain/evil -> /etc/attacker-policy).

4. index.ts: use config.toolAnnotationsDir ?? config.generatedDir for
   consistency with TrustedProcess policy loading.

5. git-sync: switch GIT_PROTOCOL_WHITELIST to GIT_ALLOW_PROTOCOL (the
   supported env var in modern git) and include http in the allowlist.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(escalation): track approved escalations and handle expiry in cron sessions

Add onEscalationResolved callback to SessionOptions, fired when an
escalation is approved or denied. Wire through AgentSession (direct
call after writing response file) and DockerAgentSession (via
EscalationWatcher). The daemon uses this to increment escalationsApproved
in RunRecord instead of leaving it hardcoded to 0.

Also wire onEscalationExpired in cron sessions so the SessionManager's
pending escalation state is cleared when the proxy times out.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(cron): exit process after run-job completes

runJobCommand never called daemon.shutdown() or process.exit(),
leaving lingering handles (logger, scheduler) that kept the
Node.js event loop alive indefinitely after job completion.

* fix(lint): resolve 7 eslint errors across cron test files

Remove unused imports (CronFields, readFileSync, JobId), unused
variables (first, l2), unnecessary optional chain, and suppress
destructuring-discard lint for _rawArgs in argument-roles.ts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(security): treat empty git path as missing; probe socket before unlink

Git path enrichment now treats empty/whitespace `path` the same as
absent, preventing resolution to CWD which could escape the sandbox.

Control socket startup now probes whether an existing socket is live
before unlinking. If a daemon is already listening, start() throws
instead of silently orphaning the running daemon.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(daemon): clear pending escalation after resolution; update review instructions

Clear pending escalation state in onEscalationResolved callback so
auto-denied escalations don't leave stale state in SessionManager.

Update types.instructions.md to reflect that write-history and
delete-history are intentionally in SANDBOX_SAFE_PATH_ROLES — they
only discharge the path component while URL roles still require
compiled rule evaluation.

---------

Co-authored-by: IronCurtain <ironcurtain@provos.org>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Remove all tmux references from captain instructions

The captain doesn't need to know about tmux. It just needs to know
its helper scripts. Removing the implementation detail eliminates the
temptation to use raw tmux commands.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update applyUpdates prompt: include manager comments, return only new/modified items

- Add labelled managerComments to user prompt
- Change sys prompt from returning full checklist to returning only
  new or modified items, so parseAIChecklist can import them directly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Tracker: avancement incrémental au lieu du recalcul destructif

- Quand REVISION/READING est coché/décoché dans la grille, la position
  avance/recule de l'objectif quotidien (±hizbPerDay)
- GET /api/progress-tracker ne modifie plus les positions (lecture seule)
- Le bouton "Actualiser" rafraîchit l'affichage sans écraser les données
- Le dashboard se met à jour après chaque toggle REVISION/READING

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: amilou
repository.description: Suivi Apprentissage

commit.message:
Fix: Explicitly disable ports in production compose override

docker-compose.prod.yml improvements:
- Add 'ports: []' to minio service (overrides base config)
- Add 'ports: []' to postgres service (overrides base config)
- Ensures no services are exposed to host in production
- API remains available through expose (reverse proxy access)

In Docker Compose, settings from multiple files are merged, not replaced.
By explicitly setting 'ports: []', we override the base docker-compose.yml
ports configuration and prevent port conflicts.

Services now:
- PostgreSQL: accessible only within Docker network
- MinIO: accessible only within Docker network
- API: accessible through expose (via Nginx reverse proxy)

This prevents 'address already in use' errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Add token cost analyzer tool for reverse-engineering subscription credit costs

Correlates transcript token usage with utilization snapshots via statistical
regression to estimate per-model token costs in terms of subscription allowance.

Supports two analysis modes:
- Raw 12-feature regression (4 token types x 3 model families)
- Weighted API-cost regression (collapses correlated features using pricing ratios)

Includes --tier 5x|20x support for Pro vs Max/Team subscriptions, configurable
aggregation windows, and cross-validation against API pricing ratios.

commit.message:
test: unit tests for openclaw gateway resilience config (#2224)

* test(e2e): add openclaw gateway kill/restart resilience test

Verifies that the openclaw gateway auto-restarts after being killed
with SIGKILL, validating the systemd Restart=always supervision.

The test runs as part of verify_openclaw:
1. Confirms gateway is listening on :18789
2. Kills it with SIGKILL (simulates a hard crash)
3. Waits up to 30s for systemd to auto-restart it
4. Verifies port 18789 comes back online

If the gateway isn't running (e.g. non-systemd env), the test is
skipped gracefully. On failure, dumps systemd status and gateway
logs for diagnostics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Revert "test(e2e): add openclaw gateway kill/restart resilience test"

This reverts commit 39b79d5c129deffadb0ad64f44fe8fcbe791b3dd.

* test: add unit tests for openclaw gateway resilience config

Verifies that startGateway() produces correct systemd and cron
configuration for auto-restart after a gateway crash:

- Restart=always and RestartSec=5 in the systemd unit
- Cron heartbeat checks port 18789 and restarts if dead
- Wrapper script sources .spawnrc and execs openclaw gateway
- Multiple port-check fallbacks (ss, /dev/tcp, nc)
- Non-systemd fallback to setsid/nohup
- 300s startup timeout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(e2e): add openclaw gateway kill/restart resilience test

Kills the gateway with SIGKILL during verify_openclaw and verifies
systemd Restart=always brings it back within 30s. Skips gracefully
on non-systemd environments.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: secrets scanner false positives on field names and docs text

  always contain these, variable names never do)

  "scan" etc. (documentation context, not actual secrets)
- Added 4 tests: apiKeyValid, doc text, detection text, real key

- const apiKeyValid = true
- "50+ patterns covering AWS, GitHub, Stripe keys"
- "AWS secret key detection"

feat: add ux-designer, ui-developer, and data-engineer agents (#62)

* feat: add ux-designer and ui-developer agents

Adds two new specialist agents:
- ux-designer (opus/coral): research-backed UX critique with cognitive
  psychology toolkit, heuristic evaluation workflow, and accessibility
  baseline enforcement
- ui-developer (sonnet/indigo): pixel-perfect UI implementation with
  design token architecture, animation performance guidance, and
  component quality checklist

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add data-engineer agent (opus)

Adds a staff-level data engineer agent with:
- Medallion architecture reference (Bronze/Silver/Gold layer responsibilities)
- Storage selection framework (columnar vs row-oriented vs NoSQL vs streaming)
- Orchestration decision framework (Airflow vs Dagster vs Prefect vs dbt)
- Pipeline failure modes with prevention and recovery strategies
- SQL/transformation review checklist (grain, idempotency, null handling, etc.)
- Idempotency as a blocking guardrail, not a guideline

North star: build systems that fail gracefully — assume the data will be
messy, the API will go down, and the schema will change.
